title: Strong TDD adherence — 105 fine-grained eunit tests, no skips
evidence: test/ has 9 modules / 105 _test() functions exercising csv, normalize, data, query, tools, mcp, server, format layers; grep for skip/ignore/disable returns none; test_coverage=1.0 in scores.json
suggestion: None — the prompt-factor instruction (test-first incremental development) is well satisfied.

title: Robustness beyond spec: cross-file fixture dedup + accent/suffix name normalization + team aliases
evidence: bsmcp_data.erl:157 dedup/1 by (date,home_norm,away_norm); bsmcp_normalize.erl normalize/1 strips state suffix and folds accents; bsmcp_aliases.erl lookup/1
suggestion: None — these handle the data-quality notes in TASK.md (name variations, encoding).

title: find_matches tool exposes season but not an explicit date-range filter
evidence: bsmcp_tools.erl:16-26 find_matches schema offers team/opponent/home_team/away_team/season/competition/limit — no from/to date args; bsmcp_query:find_matches/2 predicates (bsmcp_query.erl:24-44) have no date predicate
suggestion: R4 ('date range and/or season') is satisfied by season filtering, but add optional date_from/date_to args to fully cover the 'date range' phrasing.

title: find_players sort places players with missing Overall rating first
evidence: bsmcp_query.erl:147 sorts by `overall(A) >= overall(B)`; overall/1 (line 172) may return the atom `undefined` for blank ratings, and in Erlang term order an atom sorts higher than any integer, so unrated players would head the list
suggestion: Coerce undefined ratings to a sentinel (e.g. -1) in the sort comparator so they sink to the bottom of find_players results.
